title: Crispy British Chicken Curry
description: A spicy, crispy chicken curry with a British twist, using Madras paste and a reduction that gives a midnight-craving comfort glow.

ingredients.name: Skinless chicken breast
ingredients.notes: cut into bite-sized pieces and pat dry for crispiness

instructions: Pat the chicken dry with paper towels to help it crisp up.
Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat; add the chicken and sear until golden on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add another tbsp of oil to the same skillet. Sauté onion, garlic, and ginger until softened, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the Madras curry paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the chicken to the pan and pour in the coconut milk, stirring to combine. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the curry bubble slowly for 12-15 minutes, or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 75°C (165°F).
Stir in the Greek yogurt and lemon juice, then uncover and simmer for another 2 minutes to thicken slightly.
Season with salt to taste, sprinkle with fresh coriander, and serve immediately over rice or with a side of crusty bread.
